citeinfo.title: Cyanotoxin, chlorophyll-a, and cyanobacterial toxin genetic data for samples collected at eleven large river sites throughout the United States, June through September 2017
citeinfo.geoform: Excel Spreadsheet and Comma Separated Values (CSV) file

citeinfo.title: Cyanobacteria, cyanotoxin synthetase gene, and cyanotoxin occurrence among selected large river sites of the conterminous United States, 2017–18

descript.abstract: This U.S. Geological Survey (USGS) data release provides discretely measured cyanotoxin, chlorophyll-a, and cyanobacterial toxin genetic data for samples collected from eleven large river sites throughout the United States, from June through September 2017. Discrete water-quality samples were analyzed for cyanotoxins (anatoxin, cylindrospermopsin, microcystin, and saxitoxin), chlorophyll-a, and cyanobacterial toxin genetics as part of a National Water-Quality Assessment Project pilot study to describe potential cyanotoxin occurrence in the Nation's large rivers.
descript.purpose: These data were collected by the National Water-Quality Assessment Project as part of a pilot study to describe the potential for cyanotoxin occurrence in the Nation's large rivers. This data release was produced in support of the Inland Waters journal article by Graham and others (2020) (https://doi.org/10.1080/20442041.2019.1700749) describing cyanotoxin occurrence during June through September 2017 and in compliance with the federal open-data requirements as a way to make scientific products associated with USGS research efforts and publications available to the public.

spdom.descgeog: Data were collected from eleven sites throughout the United States: Connecticut River at Thompsonville, CT (USGS Station Identification Number 01184000); Delaware River at Trenton, NJ (USGS Station Identification Number 01463500); Susquehanna River at Conowingo, MD (USGS Station Identification Number 01578310); Chattahoochee River near Whitesburg, GA (USGS Station Identification Number 02338000); Ohio River at Cannelton Dam at Cannelton, IN (USGS Station Identification Number 03303280); Mississippi River at Hastings, MN (USGS Station Identification Number 05331580); Kansas River at De Soto, KS (USGS Station Identification Number 06892350); Missouri River at Hermann, MO (USGS Station Identification Number 06934500); Trinity River Below Dallas, TX (USGS Station Identification Number 08057410); Sacramento River at Freeport, CA (USGS Station Identification Number 11447650); and Willamette River at Portland, OR (USGS Station Identification Number 14211720).

place.placekey: Ohio River at Cannelton Dam at Cannelton, IN
Kansas River at De Soto, KS

idinfo.useconst: Users are advised to read the dataset's metadata thoroughly to understand appropriate data limitations.

cntperp.cntorg: U.S. Geological Survey, National Water-Quality Assessment Project

idinfo.datacred: This study was conducted by the U.S. Geological Survey National Water-Quality Assessment Project.

attracc.attraccr: All data were broadly checked for accuracy.

dataqual.logic: No formal logical accuracy tests were conducted, but data were checked for consistency and numerical errors.
dataqual.complete: Dataset is considered complete for the information presented, as described in the abstract. Users are advised to read the rest of the metadata record carefully for additional details.

horizpa.horizpar: No formal positional accuracy tests were conducted.

vertacc.vertaccr: No formal positional accuracy tests were conducted.

procstep.procdesc: All data were compiled into one spreadsheet for this data release.

method.methtype: Discrete water-quality sample collection and analysis.
method.methdesc: Discrete water-quality samples were collected from eleven large river sites in the United States, from June through September 2017, in accordance with U.S. Geological Survey protocols as described in Graham and others (2020). All samples (n=61) were analyzed for total cyanotoxins (anatoxins, cylindrospermopsins, microcystins, and saxitoxins) by enzyme-linked immunosorbent assay (ELISA). A subset of samples (n=6) were analyzed for total cyanotoxins (anatoxin-a, cylindrospermopsin, 10 microcystin variants, and nodularin-R) by liquid chromatography tandem-mass spectrometry (LC/MS/MS), chlorophyll-a and the degradate pheophytin-a in accordance with U.S. Environmental Protection Agency method 445.0, and genes present in anatoxin (anaC), cylindrospermopsin (cyrA), microcystin (mcyE), and saxitoxin (stxA) synthetase gene clusters by quantitative polymerase chain reaction (qPCR). Details on the standard curve for the qPCR results are given in a table associated with the metadata and described as a separate entity below. Cyanotoxins were analyzed by the USGS Organic Geochemistry Research Laboratory (Lawrence, Kansas) as described in Loftin and others (2008) and Graham and others (2010). Chlorophyll-a and pheophytin-a was analyzed at the USGS National Water Quality Laboratory (Lakewood, Colorado), in accordance with the U.S. Environmental Protection Agency Method 445.0 as described in Arar and others (1997). Cyanobacterial genetics were analyzed at the USGS Ohio Microbiology Laboratory (Columbus, Ohio) as described in Stelzer and others (2013). Molecular assays for cyanobacterial genes were done to enumerate the following: mcyE genus-specific toxin genes for microcystin (Rantala and others, 2006 and Sipari and others, 2010), sxtA toxin gene for saxitoxin (Al-Tebrineh and others, 2012), anaC toxin gene for anatoxin-a (Sabart and others, 2015) and cyrA toxin gene for cylindrospermopsin (Al-Tebrineh and others, 2012). The limit of blank (LoB), limit of detection (LoD), and limit of quantification (LoQ) were calculated for each assay according to procedures in Francy and others (2017).

citeinfo.origin: Al-Tebrineh, J., Pearson, L.A., Yasar, S.A., and Neilan, B.A.
citeinfo.title: A multiplex qPCR targeting hepato- and neurotoxigenic cyanobacteria of global significance
U.S. Environmental Protection Agency Method 445.0, In vitro determination of chlorophyll a and pheophytin a in marine and freshwater algae by fluorescence

citeinfo.origin: Francy, D.S., Bushon, R.N., Cicale, J.R., Brady, A.M.G., Kephart, C.M., Stelzer, E.A., and Ecker, C.D.
citeinfo.title: Quality assurance/quality control manual: Ohio Water Microbiology Laboratory

citeinfo.origin: Graham, J.L., Dubrovsky, N.M., Foster, G.M., King, L.R., Loftin, K.A., Rosen, B.H., and Stelzer, E.A.
citeinfo.title: Cyanotoxin occurrence in large rivers of the United States

citeinfo.origin: Graham, J.L., Loftin, K.A., Meyer, M.T., and Ziegler, A.C.
citeinfo.title: Cyanotoxin mixtures and taste-and-odor compounds in cyanobacterial blooms from the midwestern United States

citeinfo.origin: Loftin, K.A., Meyer, M.T., Rubio, F., Kamp, L., Humphries, E., and Whereat, E.
citeinfo.title: Comparison of two cell lysis procedures for recovery of microcystins in water samples from Silver Lake in Dover, Delaware, with microcystin producing cyanobacterial accumulations

citeinfo.origin: Rantala, A., Rajaniemi-Wacklin,P., Lyra, C., Lepisto, L., Rintala, J., Mankiewicz-Boczek, J., and Sivonen, K.
citeinfo.title: Detection of microcystin-producing cyanobacteria in Finnish lakes with genus-specific microcystin synthetase gene E (mcyE) PCR and associations with environmental factors

citeinfo.origin: Sabart, M., Crenn, K., Perriere, F., Abila, A., Leremboure, M., Colombet, J., Jousse, C., and Latour, D.
citeinfo.title: Co-occurance of microcystin and anatoxin-a in the freshwater lake Aydat (France): analytical and molecular approaches during a three-year survey

citeinfo.origin: Sipari, H., Rantala-Ylinen, A., Jokela, J., Oksanen, I., and Sivonen, K.
citeinfo.title: Development of a chip assay and quantitative PCR for detecting microcystin synthetase E gene expression

citeinfo.origin: Stelzer, E.A., Loftin, K.A., and Struffolino, Pamela
citeinfo.title: Relations between DNA- and RNA-based molecular methods for cyanobacteria and microcystin concentration at Maumee Bay State Park Lakeside Beach, Oregon, Ohio, 2012

serinfo.sername: U.S. Geological Survey Scientific Investigations Report
serinfo.issue: U.S. Geological Survey Scientific Investigation Report 2013-5189

spdoinfo.indspref: Connecticut River at Thompsonville, CT (01184000) Long= -72.60583 Lat= 41.98722; Delaware River at Trenton, NJ (01463500) Long= -74.77806 Lat= 40.22167; Susquehanna River at Conowingo, MD (01578310) Long= -76.17444 Lat= 39.65789; Chattahoochee River near Whitesburg, GA (02338000) Long= -84.90083 Lat= 33.47694; Ohio River at Cannelton Dam at Cannelton, IN (03303280) Long= -86.70556 Lat= 37.89944; Mississippi River at Hastings, MN (05331580) Long= -92.84778 Lat= 44.74583; Kansas River at De Soto, KS (06892350) Long= -94.96444 Lat= 38.98333; Missouri River at Hermann, MO (06934500) Long= -91.43850 Lat= 38.70981; Trinity River below Dallas, TX (08057410) Long= -96.73556 Lat= 32.70750; Sacramento River at Freeport, CA (11447650) Long= -121.5003 Lat= 38.45611; Willamette River at Portland, OR (14211720) Long= -122.6692 Lat= 45.5175

enttyp.enttypd: Standard curve information for quantitative polymerase chain reaction (qPCR) assays.
Cyanotoxin, chlorophyll-a, and cyanobacterial toxin genetic data for samples collected at eleven large river sites throughout the United States, June through September 2017.

edom.edomvds: Not Specified

attr.attrdef: USGS station name where samples were collected.

edom.edomvd: Month/Day/Year
edom.edomvds: Not Specified

attr.attrdef: Time samples were collected.

edom.edomv: HH:MM; "NA" indicates lab replicate and no time offset.
edom.edomvd: Hours:Minutes
edom.edomvds: Not Specified

attr.attrdef: Type of sample collected.

edom.edomvd: Primary environmental sample
edom.edomvds: Not Specified

edom.edomvd: Two or more samples of environmental water that are collected simultaneously or at approximately the same time in the field.
edom.edomvds: U.S. Geological Survey National Field Manual, Chapter A4

edom.edomvd: A sample collected in a single bottle that is split at the laboratory into two or more replicates after having been processed and preserved.
edom.edomvds: U.S. Geological Survey National Field Manual, Chapter A4

attr.attrlabl: Data qualifiers for Total microcystins plus nodularins
attr.attrdef: Data qualifiers for attribute labeled Total microcystins plus nodularins (ug/L)

edom.edomvd: Less than symbol indicates concentration was less than laboratory reporting level.
edom.edomvds: Kansas Water Science Center Organic Geochemistry Research Laboratory

attr.attrdef: Total microcystins plus nodularins, unfiltered water, freeze/thaw extraction, ADDA specific enzyme-linked immunosorbent assay, recoverable, micrograms per liter (USGS parameter code 89011); refer to prior attribute for associated data qualifier; "NM" indicates value was not measured.
Data qualifiers for attribute labeled Total cylindrospermopsins (ug/L).

edom.edomvd: Less than symbol indicates concentration was less than laboratory reporting level.
edom.edomvds: Kansas Water Science Center Organic Geochemistry Research Laboratory

edom.edomvd: Less than symbol indicates concentration was less than laboratory reporting level.

edom.edomvd: Less than symbol indicates concentration was less than laboratory reporting level.

attr.attrdef: Anatoxin-a, water, unfiltered, recoverable, micrograms per liter (USGS parameter code 68287); refer to prior attribute for associated data qualifier; "NM" indicates value was not measured.
Chlorophyll a, phytoplankton, chromatographic-fluorometric method, micrograms per liter (USGS parameter code 70953); "NM" indicates value was not measured.
Pheophytin a, phytoplankton, micrograms per liter (USGS parameter code 62360); "NM" indicates value was not measured.

cntaddr.address: Denver Federal Center, Building 810, Mail Stop 302

distinfo.distliab: Unless otherwise stated, all data, metadata and related materials are considered to satisfy the quality standards relative to the purpose for which the data were collected. Although these data and associated metadata have been reviewed for accuracy and completeness and approved for release by the U.S. Geological Survey (USGS), no warranty expressed or implied is made regarding the display or utility of the data on any other system or for general or scientific purposes, nor shall the act of distribution constitute any such warranty.

metainfo.metstdn: FGDC Content Standard for Digital Geospatial Metadata
